---
title: "Personal Website"
date: 2024-04-01
params:
  project-tag: personal-website
github: "https://github.com/RiversPharmD/ztr_website"
demo: "https://riverspharmd.github.io/ztr_website"
---

# What it is

This site: a home base for my projects, a place to practice coding outside work, and a way to connect with others. Built with Quarto—responsive, searchable, and easy to tweak.

# Why I did it

I wanted a single place to point people (employers, collaborators, friends) that wasn't LinkedIn or a CV. Also wanted to get comfortable with Quarto and static sites.

# What I learned (or am hoping to learn)

Quarto's flexibility (R, Python, or neither), how to keep content and structure maintainable with templates, and how to wire up a simple sidebar + search without overdoing it. Still iterating on tone and what to put in "projects" vs "posts."

# Links

- **Repo:** [GitHub](https://github.com/RiversPharmD/ztr_website)
- **Site:** [Live site](https://riverspharmd.github.io/ztr_website)

# Recent posts

Posts tagged with this project (most recent 3):

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| results: asis

tag <- params[["project-tag"]]
if (is.null(tag)) tag <- sub("\\.qmd$", "", basename(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)))

posts_dir <- "blog/posts"
files <- list.files(posts_dir, pattern = "\\.qmd$", full.names = TRUE)
files <- files[basename(files) != "_template.qmd"]

parse_frontmatter <- function(path) {
  lines <- readLines(path, warn = FALSE)
  delim <- which(lines == "---")
  if (length(delim) < 2) return(NULL)
  block <- paste(lines[(delim[1] + 1):(delim[2] - 1)], collapse = "\n")
  tryCatch(yaml::yaml.load(block), error = function(e) NULL)
}

posts <- list()
for (f in files) {
  fm <- parse_frontmatter(f)
  if (is.null(fm)) next
  tags <- fm$tags
  if (is.null(tags)) next
  if (!tag %in% tags) next
  title <- if (!is.null(fm$title)) fm$title else "Untitled"
  date  <- if (!is.null(fm$date)) fm$date else NA_character_
  stem  <- sub("\\.qmd$", "", basename(f))
  href  <- paste0("../blog/posts/", stem, ".html")
  posts <- c(posts, list(list(title = title, date = date, href = href)))
}

if (length(posts) == 0) {
  cat("No posts tagged with `", tag, "` yet.\n", sep = "")
} else {
  ord <- order(sapply(posts, function(p) p$date), decreasing = TRUE)
  posts <- posts[ord][1:min(3, length(posts))]
  for (p in posts) {
    cat("- [", p$title, "](", p$href, ")\n", sep = "")
  }
}
```
